narrative:
A front arrives from the northwest tonight. We have scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms, with coverage increasing later today and lingering into tonight. Some of these storms may be strong with frequent lightning, heavy downpours, and wind. Monday has lots of clouds, much cooler temperatures, and the potential for a few showers in the morning, especially on the west side of the Appalachians. After a dry, pleasant Wednesday, the atmosphere ramps up the warmth and humidity for the rest of this week--with a return of afternoon thunderstorms.

This week's Monday Message is now available for our subscribers. Severe weather potential for Monday, along with a return to classical summertime weather by next weekend are topics of discussion.

Afternoon and Evening Thunderstorms

Last Updated Mon June 22 at 4:16PM by Ray Russell

A front arrives from the northwest tonight. We have scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms, with coverage increasing later today and lingering into tonight. Some of these storms may be strong with frequent lightning, heavy downpours, and wind. Monday has lots of clouds, much cooler temperatures, and the potential for a few showers in the morning, especially on the west side of the Appalachians. After a dry, pleasant Wednesday, the atmosphere ramps up the warmth and humidity for the rest of this week--with a return of afternoon thunderstorms.

Forecast Discussion

We have a front arriving from the northwest tonight. Clouds increase today with warm and humid conditions. Showers and thunderstorms develop this afternoon (greatest coverage late-day into the early evening) and continue into tonight.

As the front stalls to our south Tuesday, the day will not feel very "summerish". Expect clouds (diminishing in the afternoon and Tuesday night) with morning showers possible especially on the west side of the Appalachians.

The pattern flips the switch to "full on summer" this week. Tonight's front weakens to our south, opening the door for warm, humid air to return from the south Wednesday. Wednesday is the pick of the week with seasonal temperatures and dry conditions.

The rest of the week features warm, muggy weather. A front approaches from the north this weekend. It may not reach us, but it will help spark afternoon convection. Expect at least "isolated" afternoon thundershowers Thursday. Coverage and intensity of afternoon and evening convection increase Friday. Then we settle into summertime heat with daily doses of afternoon showers and thunderstorms. You may not get "hit" every day, but they will be around every day for a while.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for June 22, 2026

The week starts stormy, but ends up like the first full week of summer should, with hit-or-miss afternoon thundershowers and very warm temperatures. It's the Monday Message, available for our subscribers.

Jun 22, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.
